original text:
Established in 1996 with an in-house casual wear brand named "Wrecker" for both women and men with just 20 machines that used to do outsourcing for washing, the Noida-based company has come a long way since its inception. The company now has 400 machines for sewing and the latest technology for all processes. With a strong focus on innovation and technology, the group believes that denim is not just a pair that you wear everyday but has a huge scope of creativity as well. Exporting denims to all of Europe, mainly Spain (Mayoral) for the past 15 years and also to Italy, Germany, Denmark and the UK, the company has a 70:30 ratio in domestic use vs export of denims. In India, the company is currently making denim for reputed brands like Being Human, Black Berry (URBAN), RedChief and WestSide (WestSport & Nuon).
Team Perfect Sourcing, in a tête-à-tête with Shailesh Jain, Director of the company lists the details of its journey.
PS: How has the journey been so far?
There have been many ups-and-downs since starting and now with hard work dedication and commitment, we feel that this growth will continue. Now we have more than 400 machines with in-house laundry facilities; washing and designing is also done within our premises.
Denim is a creative product and the market is also lucrative and growing at a very good speed. People who are making denim for mass markets have grown substantially vis-a-vis those who are making it for standard or premium markets.
PS: What are the focus products?
Consumers want best quality denims at reasonable prices, so that's what we are focussing on now. We are more into fashion and premium products and foresee substantial growth in this segment. Our focus is mainly on denims for men and kids and we feel that the growth can be 20 per cent yearly in this segment. Kids' wear comprises almost 80 per cent of our total capacity. Very recently we have started with men's wear brands like Being Human, BlackBerry (URBAN), RedChief and WestSide (WestSport & Nuon) also. Apart from jeans, denim dresses, skirts, shirts and jackets are also well received in all age groups. Jeans is becoming more of a working class culture than a fashion garment.
PS: What upgradations have been done in technology at your company?
Denim needs constant innovation and for that, technological upgrades are the need of the hour. We have all facilities in-house. Fabric & trims are what we procure and the rest all is done with in-house facilities including embroidery & all kinds of washing. Cost reduction is possible only by increasing capacities and so presently, we are working on numbers and also making IE (Industrial Engineering) department work more efficiently towards cutting down costs.
PS: What is the strategy of growth for 2019?
More machines, new laundry, compliance facilities, less buyers and more capacity.
PS: What is the monthly capacity of the company's manufacturing unit?
We have the capacity to produce 1,25,000 pieces monthly with hi-tech machinery and adhering to benchmarked quality standards. The fabrics that we have are denim 3.50 oz. to 14.50 oz., canvas, twill, poplin, voile, cambric, linen, corduroy, yarn dyed, twill lycra, satin lycra in various washes, prints & embroideries. As for denims, we have jeans, jackets, shirts, dresses, skirts, blouses, vests, tops, t-shirts, shorts, bermudas, trousers , capris, overalls, rompers and jumpers.
PS: Any expansion plans? If yes, by when will it be completed?
Yes we are planning some expansion over the year and one more factory will be coming up in Noida soon. We will be increasing our capacity in sewing and washing and focus on different washing techniques as well. We are focussing on practices and products which are eco-friendly with less water consumption needed in washing or other processes. We plan to start this expansion from June 2019.
PS: What are the challenges of the denim market and manufacturing today?
Price has been the biggest challenge for the denim market today. But in our experience, conversion of basic denim to a high fashion product will bring you out from that challenge.
PS: What is the general export market scenario prevailing at present?
At present exports orders have shrunk in number and it would be less as compared to the past two years. But the market forecast is that it will increase after 2020.
PS: What measures do you take to grow with sustainability?
Sustainability is very important to any business or else it becomes a risky affair. We have limited ourselves in buyer selection and capacity allocations for individual customers. Increasing business with regular co-partners in addition with 1-2 new partners every year has made our growth sustainable.
PS: How is your company complied with the concept of Gender Equality?
Gender equality in garment industry is very important. We pay both males and females as per Govt norms and special security is given to all women co-workers so that they feel safe. We are in association with a 3rd party NGO where our workers are in direct touch with them to get advice in case of any sexual harassment by any co-worker and internal team within the organisation. The organisation also takes updates from workers at regular intervals. Right now, the ratio of women to men is 30:70 but we hope to increase the number and make it 40:60 in next two years. We are also associated with an NGO called ‘Syadwad Yuva Club', where we are doing free health check up camps, blood donation camps, clothes distribution , education for the poor, food distribution , cleanliness drives and many other activities.
Why Is India Not Able To Reach Apparel Export Targets?
How this Indian apparel exporter is planning to bolster profits?
Can West Bengal Be The Next Garment Manufacturing Hub In India?

original text:
ALICE + OLIVIA JEANS "Roslyn" cutout shoulder and neckline tee. Crew neckline. Short sleeves. Relaxed fit. Hem hits hip. Straight hem. Pullover style. Viscose/spandex. Imported.
Alice + Olivia "Roslyn" sweater with cutout details. Crew neckline. Short sleeves. Fitted silhouette. Pullover style. Viscose/polyester. Imported.
Nicole Miller New York tee in jersey. Crew neckline; collarbone cutout details. Short sleeves. Relaxed fit. Straight hem. Pullover style. Rayon/spandex. Dry clean. Imported.
Skin-baring slashes at the neckline and shoulder add edge to a slim shell that goes easily from weekday to weekend. Style Name: Alice + Olivia Roslyn Cutout Fitted Sweater. Style Number: 5740866. Available in stores.
Awesome Redemption Church swag that helps represent the church and be able to have conversations in our Ogden, Layton, and Roy communities.
Varley "Marr" tee featuring a geometric cutout at center back. V neckline. Short sleeves. Relaxed silhouette. Banded hem. Pullover style. Supima cotton. Imported.
Terez tee with raw edges. Crew neckline with V cutout. Short sleeves. Split sides. Pullover style. Rayon/spandex. Made in USA of imported material.
Splendid Studio solid jersey T-shirt with cutout back. Scoop neckline. Long sleeves. Relaxed fit. Pullover style. Rayon/spandex. Machine wash. Imported.
cinq a sept "Alyssa" dress with shoulder cutouts. Crew neckline. Short sleeves; cutouts. Fit-and-flare silhouette. Mini length. Straight hem. Hidden back zip. Polyester/viscose/spandex. Imported.

original text:
The year is 2031. Crime is on the rise.
All across the world, dozens of seemingly unsolvable murders pop up every day, with no apparent purpose or pattern.
Until, that is, someone noticed that renowned mystery novelist Kyle DeMille had written several books with remarkable similarities to a number of the murders.
Shortly after his arrest, however, it became clear that DeMille was not acting alone. As more evidence came to light, and a number of new novels popped up under pseudonyms, the extent of the scheme became clear.
There was an entire criminal organization run by murder mystery authors conducting research to learn if their intended crimes were plausible. They called themselves Undercover, and aside from DeMille, their members proved elusive to track down.
It was only a matter of months before writing murder mysteries was banned under international treaty. Today, no author who hopes to have a serious career will admit to ever writing one, and publishing companies are cracking down on suspected authors in their ranks.
Iconoclast Publishing was no exception. They were a small publisher with only twenty-one authors under their banner, and they couldn't afford the bad press if they were found to be harboring criminals.
Tonight, Iconoclast was holding a party for all of their authors, and attendance was mandatory.
Naturally, everyone suspected the real reason for the party was to flush out the Undercover agents. They were right, but they were still in for an unpleasant surprise.
Once all the authors were in, someone wondered where the head editor was. A quick check of his office revealed the shocking truth: He was dead, crushed under his own office chair, just like the victim in A Bad Day at the Office. Undercover had struck.
Worse, the building was locked. The authors were trapped, knowing only that some among them were coldblooded killers who wrote about their crimes.
And so the race was on to find Undercover.
This is a Mafia game! Setup was handled by soundlyParanoid and flavor is being handled by me. It's going to be a pretty straightforward game, expect plenty of vanilla roles.
We're looking for twenty-one players in total. If you're taking part, we invite you to come up with a name for the author you'll be playing as. Send me a PM with the name so I can plug it into the role flavor (which I'm still writing out, but I expect to be done by Monday at the latest).
-We may suggest roleplayable quirks for you to follow based on your role flavor. This is optional, but if you keep it up and make it amusing, you may just get a prize! Prizes will be nifty but not necessarily game-changing.
2. Crowstone - Whimbrel - Rory Ringepinder, playwright, caramel townie, lynched D6.
19. Gnauga - R.L. Stine - Undercover traitor, one-shot bulletproof, had a variety of win conditions depending on if he were dead or recruited and which factions won the game.
-None on the waiting list, contact me if you're interested.
(03-07-2014, 05:56 AM)Dragon Fogel Wrote: " The authors were trapped, knowing only that some among them were coldblooded killers who wrote about their crimes.
Fogel, how dare you. This is outright libel, it's like you didn't even try to hide it.
I guess I'll join too, but only in the hopes that I might manage to find the inevitable mod self-insert character and make him answer for his crimes against me.
Para ordered me to sign up for this.
(06-03-2014, 03:10 AM)Dragon Fogel Wrote: " DON'T EDIT POSTS YOU'LL GET MODKILLED wait a minute.
can I join? I know nothing about the mafia rules but whatever. Should i write like a profile of author-me???????
(03-10-2014, 10:28 AM)Dalmationer Wrote: " can I join? I know nothing about the mafia rules but whatever. Should i write like a profile of author-me???????
There's no need for that, just give me a name and I'll give you a brief backstory with your role.
Although if you want to come up with a bunch of crazy stuff to add onto that backstory, well, I've got no problem with that.
Coldblooded has been moved off the player list and onto the replacement list due to being busy.
I will be Nell M. Frank because all my favourite scifi authors have middle names.
(03-10-2014, 05:39 PM)Dalmationer Wrote: " I will be Nell M. Frank because all my favourite scifi authors have middle names.
I meant to PM me with it, but it doesn't actually matter that much since the name won't determine anything actually relevant to the game.

original text:
Open to a "World of Possibilities"
I have traveled to many countries including France, Italy, The Netherlands, The UK, Kenya, US, Mexico, Dominican Republic, Fiji, Japan, Korea, Singapore, UAE, and most recently India. I lived in Japan for 2 1/2 years. So I have experience with all types of people in different circumstances. I enjoy exploring and don't mind being on my own for long periods of time.
I have been a single mom for 13 years so I am used to maintaining a house on my own, including doing repairs. Now that my kids have grown, I am free to get back to exploring more.
Over the years, I have rescued 4 dogs and 1 cat. My cat is now 18 but lives with my daughters. When my kids were younger we had guinea pigs, rabbits and hamsters so I have experience with different types of creatures.
I have been a responsible homeowner for 20 years so I have a different mentality than a "tenant". I will respect your home and treat it the way I would treat my own.
I have been a volunteer in Girl Guides and at my daughters' elementary school as PAC President (Parent Advisory Council), so I have had a criminal record check completed twice in the past. This is required in Canada when working with children and/or handling money - both of which I was involved with in those volunteer positions. I was Treasurer at Girl Guides and I was solely responsible for fundraising over $6,000 for the school.
UPDATE: I am currently living in Casablanca, Morocco working as an English teacher. I will be available during Christmas break and will check the school schedule for long weekends and post them shortly.
I have owned a home for the last 20 years while raising my children. Now that they are grown and ready to explore the world on their own, I want to spend some time traveling around and figuring out the next part of my journey. I don't know where I want to settle, or if I want to settle any where. So for now, I am open to all the possibilities the Universe has to offer.
I have had 4 house-sitting jobs so far in 2018 that included either cats or dogs, along with previous house-sitting posts in animal-free homes. My latest adventure was taking care of 6 dogs - all rescues - in Costa Rica. The posting in my profile says that it was to continue until December of 2018, but the dog owner decided to ship her dogs to her new home in Thailand ahead of schedule in August. This was wonderful for the dogs because they had been separated from their owner & family for 5 months and I'm sure they were happy to be back together.
During that dog/house sit, there were MANY adventures. For example, 2 of the dogs killed a porcupine (never want to go through that again - 2 hours of me & the vet pulling quills out of the anesthetized dogs). 1 dog killed a huge iguana (at least 1 metre) and dropped it on the front terrace for me. I then had to dispose of in the jungle - how lovely!!! 3 of the dogs developed Tick Fever and had to go on antibiotics. 1 dog developed Heart worm and had to go on meds for that. 1 dog became paralyzed from the waist down after jumping out of the back of the car and landing badly (happy ending, I drove him 4 hours to San Jose to a Vet that gave him shots for Sciatica and it fixed the paralysis). At one point, the house was without water for 6 days, along with regular power outages for the entire 5 months. These are just the highlights - LOL. So, after everything I went through with these dogs, I don't think there is too much that I would not be able to handle.
I am currently living in Casablanca, Morocco working as an English teacher. I will be available during Christmas break and will check the school schedule for long weekends and post them shortly.
I had to cancel my previous commitment with Linda due to unexpected circumstances.
She graciously accepted the rescheduled sit for February ( the Dec. review was supposed to be the Feb. review).
Linda was fabulous! She loves animals which was very evident upon the first meeting as well as during her sit!
All the best, Linda, in your future endeavours!!
Love from Suki and I.
Linda was an amazing match for Suki (and myself)!
She willingly arrived a day early so that we could become well acquainted with the routine and environment and spend some time getting to know each other.
Linda kept me apprised of Suki's activities via text and email which I really appreciated.
Linda left the house neat and tidy before her departure...even left the bread maker ready to go. Thanks Linda!
I would recommend Linda, and have her back, for a sit any time! Enjoy your future sits, Linda!
Linda was an amazing house and pet sitter. I had to leave the city in 24 hours and when Linda appeared as a potential sitter, I was so pleased. She is mature and thoughtful. She immediately gained my trust, and was deserving of it. She was quick to understand the apartment and the special needs of the cats, and then while we were away, she sent updates and photos, which was reassuring. And beyond that, she is a lovely person, lively and smart and helpful. I highly recommend her for anyone looking for someone to care for their pets and house.
We would be pleased to give Linda Miner a reference! She has house sat our Condo in White Rock several times and has proven to be a very reliable and competent person. She has owned her own home and is familiar with the typical infrastructure of a home/condo etc. When we returned things were as clean, neat and tidy as when we left! She is trustworthy and we had no qualms about leaving her in charge.She has also had both a cat and a dog and knows how to look after them!
I highly recommend her as a housesitter.

She has owned her own home and is familiar with the typical infrastructure of a home/condo etc. When we returned things were as clean, neat and tidy as when we left! She is trustworthy and we had no qualms about leaving her in charge.She has also had both a cat and a dog and knows how to look after them!
I highly recommend her as a housesitter.

original text:
Layers! Our word of the day! They're a great way to tackle tough projects!
My first baby was quiet and mostly self-entertaining. All he wanted was to be near me. And I'm lazy. So when I wasn't doing something out of the house with him, I sat around goofing off a lot. I eventually came up with a basic chore list, because baby = you need clean dishes and laundry sometimes, and I worked through it. It worked for a few years.
My second baby doesn't let me sit around and goof off. So now that school and jobs have started and I'm his only daytime companion, he provides the final bit of motivation I need to not sit around all day. Still, I have trouble tackling big projects. I'm great at the first 80% of almost any organization or tidying project, which of course only takes about 20% of the time. It's harder for me to do that last 20%.
So these days I work in layers. I use the UFYH of 20 minutes on/10 minutes off (although sometimes I skip the 10 minutes) and I've divided my morning up into rooms to work on. Each room gets 20 minutes a day, up to 3x a week for the heavily used rooms. And since the basic tidying I mastered with Robin generally takes no more than 10 minutes, each time I do 20 minutes of work in a room, I dig a bit further below the surface.
I'm not sure where the journey is going to take me or how long I'll keep it up. But right now, it's making a difference. Non-crucial quick little tasks that I've procrastinated on for months or years are getting done. Things are getting put away. The exhaust hood over my stove is grime-free for the first time in too long to share. It's cool.
I'm on the downward slope in writing Wolf Interval. (I have no idea if that title will stick. I like it but justifying it may be too hard.) I'm going to reach the final scene in two weeks, or three, or four. Probably not more than four, although I've given myself six. Which is good, because I have to go back through the book and add in a lot of details. I work in layers there, too. It feels like painting a picture, starting with broad swathes of color over the pencil-strokes of outline, then passing through again to add in depth, background, expression and detail. Honestly it's one of my favorite parts of writing, because it's one of the few times when I'm absolutely certain I'm improving the work.
Layers! They're how social change happens, too, you know. It's why it seems like we keep fighting the same old battles over and over again. Things improve each sweep, but there's an awful lot of grime to scrub away and sometimes you need time away in order to look at the mess with fresh eyes.
Anyhow, so I gave him one. Then I hid the bag. And I opened my second truffle behind my back. From across the room, he heard the rattle of wrapper and came running. Because I am living in a commercial. What was I supposed to do? I gave him half. And he carefully licked out the filling then offered me the melting shell. Aw. Lindor teaches sharing.

original text:
← Nasty taste in my mouth!
I opened the Soda Stream Jet model today and will be spending the next couple of weeks reviewing the product. I was extremely surprised how user-friendly this machine was right out of the box. The instructions were very easy to follow and I had the Soda Stream put together in less than a minute. I couldn't wait to start making Soda!
I filled a Soda Stream plastic bottle as described in the instruction with ice-cold water and then went about carbonating my water. WOW!!! I added the CO2 and in less than a minute the water was a sparkling mass of bubbles!
I decided to try the Sparkling Natural Black Currant Pear first. The taste was amazing, it was a real nice mix of Currant flavor with the refreshing flavor of Pear. I must say I don't think there is a negative thing to say about this one. It's also made with Pure Cane Sugar which I think gives it a clean flavor.
I then opened the Ginger Ale. I must say I've very picky about Ginger Ale. Drumroll...............Waiting..........................Waiting...........................And the verdict is a 10 out of 10 for taste! It was great and competes face to face with Canfield and Canada Dry, my two favorite brands. Soda Stream you have a great one here!
I'm going to continue reviewing the Soda Stream Jet and Soda Stream flavors for the next few weeks.
I must say so far it's holding up to its promises! Stay tuned for more!!!
This entry was posted in Soda and tagged Beverage, Cola, diet soda, drink, home brew, Pop, Soda, Soda Stream, Unique drinks. Bookmark the permalink.
Let me know how it works for you. I'm thinking of getting one at Bed, Bath and Beyond. It looks like it would work but I want to read a review before I put out the money.
I'm on day 2 and LOVE the Soda Stream machine!!!
It is actually one gadget that lives up to the hype!!!
Jessica, I would say it's worth the investment!

original text:
On Monday, April 30th, 2012 say goodbye to April and join Amra's of Raleigh for an evening of Jazz and Swing at downtown Raleigh's premiere Cigar & Cocktail Bar on Glenwood Avenue.
There is NO COVER to enjoy the evening-but PLEASE consider supporting the event by purchasing a cocktail from the bar. Monday night bar specials include $4 well drinks and $5 Cosmopolitans. Smoking is permitted. Multiple flat screens at and around the bar. Heavy smoke suckers will be turned on for the benefit of the non-smoking clientele.
MUSIC: Enjoy deejayed modern and swing era jazz from 7:00 PM until 10:30 PM (or later)! for ambiance or dancing with Deejay Frankie Hagan. Stay for the late night w/Frankie and enjoy a rich blend of classic & contemporary soul, R&B, funk, blues, and more  -  altogether, it's the coolest hipster cocktail for your Monday night. Make Amra's your destination!
FEATURE: As we bid farewell to April we pay tribute to France and Parisian themed Jazz & Swing, and its unquestionable influence on dance and a cocktail atmosphere.
DANCE CLASS: 7:00 PM Beginner Swing dancing with Frankie Hagan and Abi Leggette (FREE CLASS).
PARKING: You can park close and conveniently on the street, or directly across the road - in the parking lot at 107 Glenwood after 5 PM.
AMBIANCE: Make yourself comfortable in a beautiful up-scale cocktail bar/premium cigar store in a setting with a gorgeous, vintage speakeasy feel. Enjoy the night air?  -  there's outside seating on the patio when the weather permits. Wanna dance? - enjoy great music and a fast, dance shoe friendly hardwood floor in an atmosphere evocative of prohibition era speakeasies in Downtown Raleigh - right on trendy Glenwood Avenue - and all of this without being out too late on a work night.
What more do you need? - Great people, dancing, cocktails, cigars, food, hipster lounging, and so much more!!
Amra's is bringing jazz & swing back to Raleigh!! Be a part of it!
On Monday, April 23rd, 2012 join Amra's of Raleigh for an evening of Jazz and Swing at downtown Raleigh's premiere Cigar & Cocktail Bar on Glenwood Avenue.
There is NO COVER to enjoy the evening-but PLEASE consider supporting the event by purchasing a cocktail from the bar. Monday night bar specials include $4 well drinks and $5 Cosmopolitans. Smoking is permitted. Multiple flat screens at and around the bar. Heavy duty smoke suckers will be turned on for the benefit of the non-smoking dance clientele.
FEATURE SET: He's an exciting Triangle area deejay and dance teacher! He's the mover and shaker behind Hot Club of Durham!  -  This Monday night he spins swing hipster hotness in the 9:00 PM feature set and brings some Hot Club magic to downtown Raleigh! Who dat?  -  It's Joseph Jackson's FIRST EVER feature set at Amra's Raleigh!!

Make Amra's your destination!
DANCE LESSON: Beginner Swing dancing w/Frankie Hagan at 7:00 PM. FREE CLASS.

original text:
Western blot analysis of lysates from human ovary and human placenta tissue (from left to right), using ENG Antibody (C-term)(Cat. #AP20953c). AP20953c was diluted at 1:1000 at each lane. A goat anti-rabbit IgG H&L(HRP) at 1:10000 dilution was used as the secondary antibody. Lysates at 20ug per lane.
This ENG antibody is generated from a rabbit immunized with a KLH conjugated synthetic peptide between 554-587 amino acids from the C-terminal region of human ENG.
ENG Antibody (C-term) is for research use only and not for use in diagnostic or therapeutic procedures.
Vascular endothelium glycoprotein that plays an important role in the regulation of angiogenesis (, ). Required for normal structure and integrity of adult vasculature (). Regulates the migration of vascular endothelial cells (). Required for normal extraembryonic angiogenesis and for embryonic heart development (By similarity). May regulate endothelial cell shape changes in response to blood flow, which drive vascular remodeling and establishment of normal vascular morphology during angiogenesis (By similarity). May play a critical role in the binding of endothelial cells to integrins and/or other RGD receptors (). Acts as TGF-beta coreceptor and is involved in the TGF-beta/BMP signaling cascade that ultimately leads to the activation of SMAD transcription factors (, , , ). Required for GDF2/BMP9 signaling through SMAD1 in endothelial cells and modulates TGFB1 signaling through SMAD3 (, , ).
Major glycoprotein of vascular endothelium. Involved in the regulation of angiogenesis. May play a critical role in the binding of endothelial cells to integrins and/or other RGD receptors. Acts as TGF-beta coreceptor and is involved in the TGF- beta/BMP signaling cascade. Required for GDF2/BMP9 signaling through SMAD1 in endothelial cells and modulates TGF-beta1 signaling through SMAD3.
Bellon T.,et al.Eur. J. Immunol. 23:2340-2345(1993).
Gougos A.,et al.J. Biol. Chem. 265:8361-8364(1990).
McAllister K.A.,et al.Nat. Genet. 8:345-351(1994).

original text:
HATTIESBURG, Miss. (CBS/WJTV/AP) Three members of the University of Southern Mississippi football team who were shot during an altercation at an off-campus nightclub early Sunday morning reportedly apologized to their coach for "letting the team down."
Linebackers Martez Smith, 22, and Tim Green, 21, and defensive lineman Deddrick Jones, 23, were shot around 1:30 a.m. Sunday after a fight broke out in Remington's Hunt Club and spilled into the club's parking lot, according to Hattiesburg police.
Southern Mississippi coach Larry Fedora declined to discuss details Monday after visiting with the players who all remain hospitalized in fair condition. Smith and Jones apparently spoke to Fedora, but the coach says Green wrote his message because he was unable to speak due to his injuries.
No arrests have been made and police have not released any possible suspects; however, Hattiesburg police spokesman Sgt. Allen Murray said the shooting was possibly gang related.
"We will do all we can to assist these young men and their families, as well as assist the Hattiesburg Police Department in its investigation of this event," said University of Southern Mississippi President Martha Saunders, according to CBS affiliate WJTV.
Smith, a fifth-year senior interdisciplinary studies major, was the team's starting middle linebacker before a season-ending knee injury last month. Jones, a junior from Bastrop, La., has started all 10 games on the defensive line, making 26 tackles this season. Green has made four tackles in the seven games he has played this season and he too is a junior interdisciplinary studies major.
"During this difficult time our thoughts and prayers are with these three members of our Southern Miss family, Martez, Tim and Deddrick, as well as their families," Fedora said in a statement released by the school. "These three men are an integral part of the success of our football program and we will continue to keep them in our hearts and hope for a speedy recovery."
Individuals with information are asked to call Crime Stoppers at 601.582.STOP or 601.582.7867. All information given to Crime Stoppers is confidential.
